instruction: Show how RAII provides exception safety when acquiring multiple resources. Write a function that opens a file, locks a mutex, and allocates memory — all exception-safe without any try/catch.
Write a scope_guard class in C++ that executes a cleanup lambda on scope exit, whether normal or exceptional. Make it movable but not copyable.
Implement a C++ RAII wrapper for a POSIX file descriptor with a custom deleter using unique_ptr. Handle error cases in the constructor.
Explain the Rule of Five in C++ with a complete implementation. When should you use Rule of Zero instead?
Show how to use weak_ptr to break a circular reference memory leak in C++. Explain the cycle and how weak_ptr solves it.
Write a thread-safe RAII resource pool in C++ where handles are automatically returned to the pool when they go out of scope.
What is enable_shared_from_this in C++ and when do you need it? Show a real use case.
Show the strong exception safety guarantee using the copy-and-swap idiom in C++.
Explain std::move — what does it actually do, and what are the common mistakes when using it?
What is the difference between std::move and std::forward? When do you use each?
Show how move semantics improve std::vector performance. Write a class that tracks copies and moves to demonstrate.
Explain Return Value Optimization (RVO) and Named RVO in C++. When does the compiler skip the copy/move?
Why must move constructors be noexcept in C++? What breaks if they aren't?
Show the moved-from state in C++. What can you safely do with an object after std::move?
Show how to implement a perfect forwarding factory function in C++ that constructs any type with any arguments.
Explain const correctness in C++: const member functions, const references, const pointers vs pointer-to-const, and the mutable keyword.
Show const and non-const overloads of operator[] in C++. How do you avoid code duplication between them?
Why can't you move from a const object in C++? Show the overload resolution.
Demonstrate how const propagates through C++ templates and auto. Show const auto&, decltype(auto), and std::as_const.
Implement a copy-on-write string in C++ using shared_ptr. Show how writes are deferred until mutation.
Write a C++ class that wraps a C library handle using RAII. The C API provides create/destroy/use functions.
Explain exception safety levels in C++ and show how to write a strongly exception-safe container insert operation.
Show std::exchange and how it simplifies move constructors and move assignment operators in C++.
Write a C++ lock_guard equivalent from scratch. Show how RAII makes mutex locking exception-safe.
Show how to use std::unique_ptr with an array and with a custom allocator in C++.
Implement a self-assignment safe move assignment operator in C++. Why does self-assignment matter for moves?
Show how to implement a type-erased RAII wrapper in C++ that can hold any resource type.
Explain the difference between unique_ptr and shared_ptr performance characteristics in C++. When should you use each?
Show how to make a C++ class non-copyable and non-movable, and explain when you'd want this.
Write a C++ unique_ptr implementation from scratch with a custom deleter template parameter.
Show how to write exception-safe code that acquires a database transaction, performs operations, and commits or rolls back using RAII.
Show how C++ RAII handles cleanup of multiple resources acquired in sequence when one fails mid-way.
How do you transfer unique_ptr ownership between functions in C++? Show factory, sink, and through patterns.
Show how move semantics interact with inheritance in C++. How do you write move constructors for derived classes?
Write a constexpr-compatible RAII guard for compile-time resource management in C++20.
Show how to safely return references from getters in C++ with proper const correctness.
Show how to implement the PIMPL idiom with unique_ptr in C++ for ABI stability and compile-time isolation.
Show self-assignment pitfalls in C++ copy assignment and how copy-and-swap eliminates them.
Write a producer-consumer queue in C++ using condition_variable and RAII locking. Show proper shutdown signaling.
How does const interact with pointers to member functions in C++? Show the full type syntax.
Show how to use std::scoped_lock in C++ to lock multiple mutexes without deadlock.
Demonstrate the difference between shallow copy and deep copy in C++ with smart pointers.
Show how to write a move-aware swap function and explain why it matters for generic algorithms.
Show how to implement a compile-time checked strong typedef using move semantics in C++.
Show how to use std::shared_ptr with weak_ptr for a thread-safe observer pattern in C++.
Show how RAII interacts with C++20 coroutines. How do you ensure cleanup in a coroutine frame?
Show move semantics with std::map and std::unordered_map in C++: insert, emplace, extract, and merge.
Demonstrate how to use mutable lambdas with move-captured variables in C++.
Write a C++ memory-mapped file wrapper using RAII that works on both Linux and Windows.
How does const_cast work in C++ and when is it safe to use? Show legal and undefined behavior examples.
Show how to implement a thread-safe singleton in C++ using RAII and static local variables (Meyers' Singleton).
Show the difference between std::lock_guard, std::unique_lock, and std::scoped_lock in C++. When to use each.
Show how to write exception-safe swap for custom types and why noexcept matters.
Show how to build an exception-safe stack with strong guarantee push and pop in C++.
Write an RAII-based connection manager in C++ that automatically retries on failure and releases on scope exit.
Explain how to safely forward captured variables in C++ lambdas using init-capture and perfect forwarding.
Implement a C++ unique_function (move-only std::function alternative) that can store move-only callables.
Show how to use std::exchange to implement a clean state machine with move-based state transitions in C++.
Show how constexpr if and const member functions interact with template specialization in C++17.
Show how to write a C++ allocator-aware container that properly handles stateful allocators with move semantics.
Show the difference between passing unique_ptr by value, by reference, and by const reference in C++ function signatures.
Write a C++ defer macro using RAII that works like Go's defer statement for one-off cleanup.
Show how to implement a reference-counted handle with custom release semantics in C++, using shared_ptr's aliasing constructor.
Demonstrate const-correct iterators in C++ and how begin/cbegin interact with range-based for.
Write a C++ class that uses RAII for transaction-like commit/rollback semantics with multiple operations.
Explain how to prevent dangling references from C++ structured bindings with auto&.
Show how to use make_unique and make_shared versus raw new in C++, and explain why make_* functions are safer.

Write a function in C++ that acquires three resources — a heap buffer, a mutex lock, and a file handle — and show exactly what happens to each resource when an exception is thrown after acquiring only two of them.
Explain the three exception safety guarantees in C++ with concrete code examples showing the differences between basic, strong, and no-throw.
Show how noexcept affects C++ function overload resolution and conditional noexcept with type traits.
Implement a C++ optional-like class that demonstrates proper move semantics and const correctness for a discriminated union.
Show how to write exception-safe assignment in C++ without using the copy-and-swap idiom. Compare the approaches.
Show how to combine RAII with C++ exception handling to implement a multi-phase initialization that cleans up partially constructed state.
Show the subtle difference between auto&& and const auto& in C++ range-based for loops with move semantics.
What is the copy elision guarantee in C++17 and how does it differ from optional NRVO? Show examples where each applies.
Show how to build a safe C++ API that prevents use-after-move at compile time using type state patterns.
Show how to implement a small buffer optimization (SBO) in C++ with proper move semantics and RAII.
Explain how to prevent slicing when copying polymorphic objects in C++. Show the clone pattern with unique_ptr.
